results.text:
y directly reflects semantic similarity.

For semantic search, query and document embeddings are precomputed. At query time, the query embedding is computed and cosine similarity is measured against all document embeddings. FAISS enables sub-linear search with approximate methods for large corpora. Domain-specific embedding models often outperform general-purpose models on specialized corpora.

Sentence embeddings are dense vector representations that capture the semantic meaning of text in a continuous high-dimensional space. Semantically similar sentences are mapped to nearby vectors, enabling efficient semantic similarity computation.

The sentence-transformers library (SBERT) provides pretrained models optimized for sentence-level semantic similarity. Models like all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (384 dimensions, 22M parameters) offer an excellent balance of speed and quality for English text. all-mpnet-base-v

, 22M parameters) offer an excellent balance of speed and quality for English text. all-mpnet-base-v2 (768 dimensions) provides higher quality.

SBERT trains using a siamese network structure with contrastive learning objectives. The model encodes two sentences and optimizes cosine similarity to be high for semantically related pairs and low for unrelated pairs. This produces embeddings where cosine similarity directly reflects semantic similarity.

steps.output_preview: Sentence embeddings are dense vector representations that capture the semantic meaning of text, enabling efficient computation of semantic similarity between sentences. In a semantic search system, qu…

final_answer: Sentence embeddings are dense vector representations that capture the semantic meaning of text, enabling efficient computation of semantic similarity between sentences. In a semantic search system, query and document embeddings are precomputed for efficiency. The cosine similarity between the query embedding and each document embedding is then measured to determine relevance. FAISS can be used for sub-linear search in large corpora, and domain-specific models often outperform general-purpose ones on specialized corpora. The sentence-transformers library (SBERT) provides pretrained models optimized for sentence-level semantic similarity, with the all-MiniLM-L6-v2 model offering a good balance of speed and quality for English text, while all-mpnet-base-v2 provides higher quality. These embeddings enable semantically similar sentences to be mapped to nearby vectors in a continuous high-dimensional space, which directly reflects their semantic similarity.
